target.title: Require evidence artifacts for run proofs
target.body:
## Summary

- add repeatable `--require-artifact <glob>` to `crabbox run` so a command that exits 0 can still fail when required proof files are missing
- collect required artifacts through the existing run artifact tarball path and reject unsupported delegated/native targets clearly
- document the new evidence guard in the run, artifact, delegated provider, and feature docs
- add a Data Runs design plan that frames data work as a generic run-evidence pattern first, with a future `crabbox data` namespace only if the generic primitives prove too awkward
- link forward-looking plans from the docs index

## Context

The framing is intentionally generic. Public `crabbox.sh` describes Crabbox as remote execution with proof, and the OpenClaw-facing docs keep the agent contract around typed `run`/`warmup` tools rather than a domain-specific data product. `--require-artifact` supports data-run manifests, QA reports, and agent proof loops without making Crabbox own connectors, schedules, raw data, or warehouse semantics.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/225

Follow-up dataflow/ETL slices are tracked separately so this PR stays scoped to the generic evidence primitive:

- https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/227
- https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/228
- https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/229
- https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/230

## Real Behavior Proof

Real SSH target setup: built the PR CLI, started a disposable Debian container running OpenSSH with `git`, `rsync`, and `tar`, then ran `crabbox run --provider ssh` against that container through the normal static SSH provider.

Successful required-artifact run:

```text
using static target lease=static_127-0-0-1 slug=127-0-0-1 target=linux host=127.0.0.1 keep=false
running on 127.0.0.1 mkdir -p reports/data ...
real ssh proof emitted
required artifact reports/data/manifest.json matched=1
artifact kind=artifact-glob path=/tmp/.../work-success/.crabbox/runs/static_127-0-0-1/static_127-0-0-1-artifacts.tgz bytes=201
run summary sync=0s command=96ms total=173ms sync_skipped=true exit=0
{"provider":"ssh","leaseId":"static_127-0-0-1","exitCode":0,"leaseStopped":true,...}
```

The resulting artifact tarball contained the bounded proof files:

```text
reports/data/manifest.json
reports/data/quality.txt
```

Missing required-artifact run against the same real SSH target. The command exits zero but omits the required manifest:

```text
running on 127.0.0.1 mkdir -p reports/data ...
real ssh command exited zero without proof
missing required artifact: reports/data/missing-manifest.json
run summary sync=0s command=83ms total=110ms sync_skipped=true exit=7 command_phases=user-command:82ms blocked_stage=unknown retry_likely=unknown
failure-bundle local=.crabbox/captures/static_127-0-0-1-20260607T194110Z.tar.gz bytes=1050 secret_risk=caller-redacts-before-sharing
{"provider":"ssh","leaseId":"static_127-0-0-1","exitCode":7,"leaseStopped":true,...}
require artifacts: exit status 1: missing required artifact: reports/data/missing-manifest.json
```

Post-check from the missing-artifact proof:

```text
REAL_SSH_DOWNLOAD_WRITTEN=no
```

That proves the real SSH-backed `run` path fails after a successful command when required proof is absent, before caller-requested `--download`, while still producing normal timing/failure lifecycle output.

## Verification

- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- real Docker-backed SSH success proof above
- real Docker-backed SSH missing-artifact proof above
- `tar -tzf <success-artifacts.tgz>`
- `go test ./internal/cli ./internal/providers/islo`
- `go test ./...`
- `scripts/check-docs.sh`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: zozo123
